text:
3. Applicant claims exemption(s) from attendance at a Mediation Information and
Assessment Meeting (MIAM)
To be completed by the person intending to make a court application or their
legal representative. Where it is required, evidence of the claimed MIAM
exemption(s) must be submitted to the court alongside this application.
If you are unable to provide the required evidence with your application, please
use the text box on page 14 to explain the reason(s) why.
The applicant has not attended a
Domestic abuse (you must complete section 3a)
MIAM because the following MIAM
exemption(s) applies:
Urgency (you must complete section 3b)
Previous attendance of a MIAM or non-court dispute resolution (you must
complete section 3c)
Other (you must complete section 3d)
Now complete the relevant section 3a, b, c or d by ticking the
appropriate box(es)
Further details of MIAM If you have claimed a MIAM exemption above you must also tick the relevant
box(es), as shown below to confirm that you have the necessary evidence
exemption(s) claimed by the
to support your ground(s) for exemption. Where required, you must submit
applicant
this evidence with your application to court. Where you are asked to provide
additional details you must do so.
Section 3a - Domestic abuse The applicant confirms that there is evidence of domestic abuse, as
evidence specified below:
This MIAM exemption requires
the evidence you have selected evidence that a prospective party has been arrested for a relevant domestic
to be submitted along with your abuse offence;
application.
evidence of a relevant police caution for a domestic abuse offence;
If you are unable to provide this, evidence of relevant criminal proceedings for a domestic abuse offence which
please use the text box on page have not concluded;
14 to explain the reason(s) why.
evidence of a relevant conviction for a domestic abuse offence;
a court order binding a prospective party over in connection with a domestic
abuse offence;
a domestic violence protection notice issued under section 24 of the Crime
and Security Act 2010 against a prospective party;
a domestic abuse protection notice given under section 22 of the Domestic
Abuse Act 2021 against a prospective party;
a relevant protective injunction;

metadata.title: Give notice of your intention to proceed with an application for a financial order: Form A
metadata.description: Use this form to start a request for a financial order in proceedings for divorce or ending a civil partnership.
form_fields: Date of the Authorised Family Mediator's signature - DD
Date of the Authorised Family Mediator's signature - MM
Date of the Authorised Family Mediator's signature - YYYY
Family mediator informed exemption from MIAM - no
Family mediator informed exemption from MIAM - yes
If CSA calculation made: state reasons
Intend to proceed with the application
Intent to apply to discharge a periodical payments order
Intent to apply to the court
Not attended MIAM exemption: domestic violence
Not attended MIAM exemption: previous attendance or exemption
Periodical payments application: for a stepchild or stepchildren
Periodical payments application: in addition to child support maintenance already paid under a Child Support Agency assessment
Periodical payments application: post-April 1993 written agreement
Periodical payments application: pre-April 1993 written agreement
Periodical payments application: to meet expenses arising from a childs disability
Periodical payments application: to meet expenses incurred by a child in being educated or training for work
Periodical payments application: when either the child or the person with care of the child or the absent parent of the child is not habitually resident in the United Kingdom
Service details: not represented, receiving advice
Type of application to discharge: lump sum
Type of application to discharge: pension compensation sharing order
Type of application to discharge: pension sharing order
Type of application to discharge: property adjustment order
Type of application: consent order - no
Type of application: consent order - yes
Type of order: pension compensation sharing order
Type of order: pension sharing order
Type of order: periodical payments order together with other financial provision
Type of order: property adjustment order
Type of order: settlement or a transfer of property for the benefit of the child(ren)
in connection with matrimonial or civil partnership proceedings
the court has granted you a decree absolute/final order
the court has granted you a decree nisi/conditional order
the court has not granted you a decree absolute/final order
the court has not granted you a decree nisi/conditional order
under paragraphs 1 or 2 of Schedule 1 to the Children Act 1989
